summary: Implement Phase 3D Retry + Dead-Letter Governor (deterministic) for emission promotion and dispatch failures: add lcs.retry_queue SUPPORTING table with unique active-item constraint, extend lcs.lcs_errors retry eligibility fields, add idempotent fn_enqueue_retry and SKIP LOCKED fn_process_retry_batch, expose v_dead_letter_queue, and enforce lcs_worker EXECUTE-only function-mediated access with tests.

payload.objective: Standardize retry scheduling, attempt counting, and dead-letter quarantine for emission promotion failures and dispatch failures.

classification.rationale: Feature work within LCS DB control-plane governance surfaces. Adds retry orchestration tables/functions/view and error metadata without modifying protected CTB backbone, altitude hierarchy, or constitutional doctrine assets.

constraints: Schema/functions/view/index/role changes via migrations under migrations/
No changes outside allowed paths without a new WORK_PACKET

create_supporting_table.name: lcs.retry_queue
create_supporting_table.model: Append-only identity, mutable workflow metadata only
columns: retry_id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()
entity_id uuid not null (emission_id OR mid)
retry_strategy text not null (IMMEDIATE | BACKOFF_5M | BACKOFF_1H | BACKOFF_1D)
next_attempt_at timestamptz not null default now()
status text not null default PENDING (PENDING | IN_PROGRESS | SUCCEEDED | DEAD)
created_at timestamptz not null default now()
updated_at timestamptz not null default now()
constraints: Partial unique index on (entity_type, entity_id) where status in (PENDING, IN_PROGRESS) to enforce one active retry item per entity
CTB register as SUPPORTING using repo-existing registry table

modify_errors_retry_eligibility.contract: Extend or enforce retry eligibility fields
fields: is_retryable boolean not null default false
modify_errors_retry_eligibility.rule: If fields already exist, enforce contract semantics instead of duplicating columns

create_queue_insert_function.name: lcs.fn_enqueue_retry
create_queue_insert_function.signature: lcs.fn_enqueue_retry(p_entity_type text, p_entity_id uuid, p_error_id uuid, p_retry_strategy text, p_max_attempts int default 5) returns uuid

create_retry_tick_function.name: lcs.fn_process_retry_batch
must: Select PENDING rows where next_attempt_at <= now()
Mark each row IN_PROGRESS and increment attempt_count
Route MID -> lcs.fn_mark_mid_ready(entity_id) by default (do not assume provider callback record path unless explicitly supported)
On success set status = SUCCEEDED and updated_at
On failure set status = DEAD when attempt_count >= max_attempts else return to PENDING and compute next_attempt_at via strategy escalation
Return summary JSON with attempted, succeeded, dead, remaining_pending

create_dead_letter_view.name: lcs.v_dead_letter_queue
create_dead_letter_view.definition: Select from lcs.retry_queue where status = DEAD with joined error context

grants: Grant EXECUTE on lcs.fn_enqueue_retry to lcs_worker
Grant EXECUTE on lcs.fn_process_retry_batch to lcs_worker
restrictions: Revoke table rights on lcs.retry_queue from lcs_worker (function-mediated only)
Revoke direct rights on related LCS control tables where needed for function-only access pattern

src.trigger_context: promotion/dispatch operation fails and is logged with a retryable error type; worker calls DB function that selects retry-eligible items and advances attempts forward-only.

builder_outputs_expected: Migration SQL for lcs.retry_queue, partial unique index, and CTB SUPPORTING registration entry
Migration SQL extending/enforcing lcs.lcs_errors retry fields
Migration SQL for lcs.fn_enqueue_retry(...) and lcs.fn_process_retry_batch(...)
Permission/grant/revoke SQL for lcs_worker function-mediated access
Enforcement tests for idempotent enqueue, batch retry processing, dead-letter transitions, and permissions

include: retry_queue exists with expected columns and SUPPORTING CTB registration
Partial unique index enforces one active retry item per entity for PENDING/IN_PROGRESS
lcs_errors retry eligibility fields exist or are contract-enforced
fn_enqueue_retry is idempotent and returns retry_id
fn_enqueue_retry computes deterministic next_attempt_at from strategy values
fn_process_retry_batch selects eligible PENDING rows with deterministic ordering and SKIP LOCKED semantics (as testable)
fn_process_retry_batch marks IN_PROGRESS, increments attempt_count, routes by entity_type, and updates status transitions deterministically
Failures go DEAD at max_attempts else return to PENDING with recomputed next_attempt_at via strategy escalation
fn_process_retry_batch returns JSON summary with required keys
v_dead_letter_queue exposes DEAD retry rows with joined error context
lcs_worker has EXECUTE on retry functions and no direct retry_queue table rights (where testable)
No RAISE-after-DML behavior in enqueue/process functions

acceptance_criteria: lcs.retry_queue exists with deterministic retry workflow fields and unique active-item constraint
lcs.lcs_errors exposes retry eligibility contract fields (added or enforced)
lcs.fn_enqueue_retry(...) exists, is idempotent, computes next_attempt_at deterministically, and returns retry_id
lcs.fn_process_retry_batch(...) exists, uses SKIP LOCKED + deterministic ordering, and advances retry attempts/status forward-only with JSON summary result
lcs.v_dead_letter_queue exists and exposes DEAD items with error context
lcs_worker can call retry functions but cannot write retry_queue directly
Tests exist and verify retry scheduling, idempotency, dead-letter behavior, and permissions
